source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Structural Damage to Crane Boom

event.top_event: Structural Damage to Crane Boom Heel
actions_taken: Crane boom was lowered to boom rest
Employees raised crane boom to take photos for investigation
Crane was repaired and placed back in service on May 2, 2017
BSEE Lafayette District conducted onsite investigation on May 10, 2017
event.summary: On April 28, 2017 at 1651 hours, the boom heel section of a Titan 5400 crane was damaged during compressor equipment transfer operations. The crane operator bypassed the boom angle limiter (kick-out) safety system to complete additional lifts at maximum vertical angle, causing the boom to contact the boom stop and sustain severe damage. The operator failed to follow Job Safety Analysis procedures and did not stop operations to discuss the safety system override.
recommendations: BSEE Lafayette District office made no recommendations to the Regional Office of Incident Investigations

threats.name: Crane operator non-compliance with JSA procedures
Boom positioned at maximum vertical angle without safety constraint

consequences.name: Risk of personnel injury from damaged equipment operation
Risk of major equipment damage from continued crane operation

controls.name: Boom Angle Limiter (Kick-out) Safety System
controls.barrier_role: Automatic stop mechanism to prevent boom from reaching unsafe angles
controls.lod_basis: API 2C 13.1.1 Boom Angle Limiters and Shut-off Devices

supporting_text: The CO bypassed the kick out to complete the additional lifts
Overriding the kick out allowed the CO to raise the boom at the desired angle without the safety device preventing the boom from coming in contact with the boom stop
Once the safety system is bypassed by a person then the person is ultimately taking the responsibility for the safety system

controls.name: Job Safety Analysis (JSA)
controls.barrier_role: Procedural control to identify and mitigate hazards before operations
controls.lod_basis: Standard operational procedure for lifting operations

supporting_text: A Job Safety Analysis (JSA) was completed for the lifts involved which specified 'boom angles too high or too low'
The CO failed to follow the safety guidelines documented on the JSA
Lessee recognized the potential hazard of lifting the boom too high on the job safety analysis but failed to perform the actual lift as per job safety analysis

controls.name: Boom Damage Inspection and Crane Removal from Service
controls.barrier_role: Detection and isolation of damaged equipment to prevent further harm

supporting_text: Prior to reusing the crane, employees noticed severe damage on the heel section of the boom
Due to the severity of the damage, the crane should have remained out of service until a certified crane mechanic was able to assess the damages
The crane was repaired and placed back in service on May 2, 2017
Crane operator failed to recognize the damaged boom after the occurrence

controls.name: Management of Change (MOC) Procedure
controls.barrier_role: Procedural control to assess and approve changes to operational parameters

supporting_text: Bypassing the boom kick-out override safety system introduced a risk to the operation that was not discussed in the JSA
Prior to bypassing this safety device the job should have been stopped and this step discussed and/or added to the JSA
Anytime an operational component changes during the present operation, the present operation must be stopped and adjusted to include the operational change

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
